plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to eliminate that risk.
Trim semiconductor exposure and reduce broad index concentration.
Increase allocation to income-generating ETF for stability.
Add a high-quality energy stock for diversification.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for safety.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% monthly gain. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, aiming for lower drawdowns.

plan: Eliminate the highest-risk leveraged ETF.
Trim semiconductor exposure and slightly reduce income ETF.
Reduce concentration in the broad index ETF.
Add two high-scoring software and fintech names.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets approximately 2.5% monthly gains by reducing leverage and concentration. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech and new additions, aiming for risk-first growth.

plan: Sell the most volatile leveraged ETF.
Trim semiconductor exposure and reduce broad index holdings.
Increase position in the strongest AI/Software name.
Add a leveraged broad index ETF for amplified gains.
Add a high potential software stock for growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly gains by increasing leverage and concentration in strong performers. It accepts higher potential drawdowns and the daily decay of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Sell all high-beta single stocks and leveraged ETFs.
Significantly increase allocation to income-focused ETFs.
Increase broad index ETF for stability and distributions.
Prioritize reliable monthly income over growth.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer for safety.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets approximately 1% monthly distributions with very low drawdown. It shifts entirely to income and broad index ETFs, minimizing volatility and growth potential.
